top_categories.workout_context: 13,878ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
161ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
13,878ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 86, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel's Paved Dolomite mountain roads, UNESCO World Heritage scenery forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 86 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 86 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 13,878ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Dolomite mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 13,878ft of climbing on paved Dolomite mountain passes does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel’s 86 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel with 13,878ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Summit temperatures 10-15C cooler than valley floors.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 86 miles of paved dolomite mountain passes in La Villa / Corvara, Alta Badia, South Tyrol, Italy.
